item.title: Re: writing
item.content:encoded:
What typos? It's absolutely riddled with them, to the point that it appears very little (human-performed) proofreading has occurred and I'd guess it was written by a person to whom English is not their first language. I didn't even make it a quarter of the way through before spotting "properties" as "proprieties", what should be "eigth" spelled as "eight", numerous instances of "on" that should be "in"… It's too much to expect a reader to list all the errors, this is why proofreading exists.

The number of previous comments complimenting the writing of the article makes me seriously question if someone other than the author has been editing this SCP without permission, and because of that I'm not going to vote. It seems like it could be a solid SCP but the frequent errors make it difficult to enjoy.

item.title: Re: writing
item.content:encoded: What typos? It would be helpful to the author to point out at least a couple. I personally can't see any when skim-reading, but it's possible I missed them.

item.title: writing
item.content:encoded:
Hello,

Congrats on writing our first SCP! I have yet to do it myself, but I thought I’d chime in.

There are numerous grammatical errors throughout the SCP, some of which appear to be simple typo’s.

It was a fun read, but the writing style didn’t seem to fit with the rest of the SCP Foundation which at first put me off. I’m glad I read through it, but I’m sure that many people simply move on after the first few paragraphs. It doesn’t seem to capture the reader as well as it should.

Best of luck!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who looked at the nickname and thought there would be a Portal reference? I did!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, it's a Foundation test facility that started issuing arbitrary 'tests' of its own after an unidentified anomalous event?

…neat. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm grateful for the feedback, Since then I tried to fix some problems you pointed out, like trying to trim down the number of black boxes in the article.
For what you said about document 2 I am currently workinging to make it seem more believable. That said I still don't have a good enough grasp to the boundries on where should I use clinical tone and where should I use a more informal conversation.
I will also consider removing the amount of effects the skip can cause. I originally thought of the idea of a random punishment to make the skip's effects unexpected just like the orders the skip gives. However, as I said, if this idea hurts the writing in itself then I will fix that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the feedback. I've changed the mistakes you pointed out.
Of course I'm interested, any suggestion is welcome.
Also, I'm glad someone found that small foreshadowing that I hid in the skip :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I sincerely enjoyed this SCP, and I upvoted it, there's a lot of writing issues, e.g. proofreading and style issues, with it. I'll point out some of the ones I saw, but there's a lot that could be improved.

1. "The newly selected Class-D personnel is to be inoculated with a very small explosive charge of remote activation inside thoracic chamber prior their entry. " Inoculated refers to vaccines. You probably meant injected. "of remote activation" is very strange. Better would be "that can be remotely activated."

2. "the need to rotate the Class-D personnel has been deemed null as long as the previous procedures are being followed." "has been deemed null" is strange - null isn't used that way. "Null" could be replaced by "unnecessary".

3."The current observed radius outside SCP-3509 for this effect to take place are 5. ██ meters." "are" should be "is"

4. "Any attempts to breach the underground complex by any other means have ended on failure." "on" should be "in".

5. "intern hemorrhage." "intern" should be "internal"

Also, the writing could be much more clinical in a number of places. I'd be happy to go in to more detail if you're interested.

This is a really good SCP idea, and it will be a very good SCP with some polishing. My favorite part was the realization of why the Junior Researcher committed suicide rather than tap dance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sure, go for it. As long as the change doesn't alter a sentence's meaning I'm fine with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a fun idea but I have a lot of issues with this.

1. the amount of black boxes, redaction's and expungement and their placement makes it feel like they're thrown in and that what is behind them isn't actually known. The don't feel very natural especially when you have:

melting of the limbs and torso, decomposition of their body as they are still alive, transformation of their living tissue into a glass-like substance, the continuous generation of metal sharpnel inside the body and [DATA EXPUNGED]

2. I feel the multitude of effects that can happen in 3509-B is a bit overkill. I think one of these effects would suffice, having multiple effect that can happen seems a bit unnecessary and doesn't really add that much to the article.

3. Addendum 3509-01 honestly makes me wonder if this article wouldn't work better as a joke scip. This part was the best bit for me as I enjoyed seeing all the things the SCP ordered and how the foundation had to scramble to fulfill them.

4. Document 2 bothers me. I really don't feel like the Junior Researcher is an actual person giving actual dialogue. It feels more like he's just giving exposition. I think it's down to the way he talks and the fact he doesn't seem panicked in any way. Lines such as "One after another I saw my colleagues and friends succumb and die to SCP-3509-B." just don't sound like something any normal person would say, it sounds like maybe an internal monologue or something someone would write in a dramatic letter, not someone who has literally jsut had his legs disappear and is now locked in an SCP.

Overall the idea is fun and the writing itself is fine, I think the tone is a bit all over the place though, too many redactions, expungements, black boxes and I really don't like the dialogue towards the end.

-1 for now but I really hope this gets re-written and comes back as it is an enjoyable idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congratulations on successfully writing your first scp, it was definitely entertaining to read. I'd like to ask if it's okay for me to fix some of the grammatical errors and poor wording in the page (there's not much of it).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great skip. Well written and entertaining as hell. My theory is that the facility voice is like a sadistic perversion of the orders given to the D-Class under normal circumstances. If this is your first, I can't wait to read your second!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the second edition of this as my first SCP. Thanks to hydrozen, fieldstone, mayoculpa in the forums as well as hyperfluid and drblackbox in the chat for the feedback given and helping me improve this.

The image is a slight modification of the one borrowed from here

Original Source:: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Engineering_Control_Center_of_the_USCGC_Mackinaw_(WLBB-30).jpg under the Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 4.0 International license.
Creator Steven Fine.
